ego: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eggon.
eight: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 8.
gie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Guébie.
git: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gitxsan.
got: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Gothic.
gout: noun. An extremely painful inflammation of joints, especially of the big toe, caused by a metabolic defect resulting in the accumulation of
gut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Maléku Jaíka.
hit: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Hittite.
hoe: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Horom.
hog: noun. Any animal belonging to the Suidae family of mammals, especially the pig, the warthog, and the boar.
hot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Hote.
how: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Honi.
howe: noun. Alternative form of how (a tumulus, a bowl barrow).
hut: noun. A small, simple one- storey dwelling or shelter, often with just one room, and generally built of readily available local materials.
outweigh: verb. To exceed in weight or mass.
owe: verb. To be under an obligation to give something back to someone or to perform some action for someone.
teg: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Teke-Tege.
tew: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tewa.
the: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chitwania Tharu.
thew: noun. An attractive physical attribute; also, physical, mental, or moral strength or vigour.
thug: noun. A person who is a member of a gang or criminal organization.
toe: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Tomedes.
tog: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tonga (Malawi).
togue: noun. Lake trout, Salvelinus namaycush, a freshwater char of northern North America.
twig: noun. A small thin branch of a tree or bush.
two: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 2.
ugh: interjection. Used to express repugnance, disgust, or annoyance.
weigh: verb. To determine the weight of an object.
weight: noun. The downwards force an object experiences due to gravity.
wet: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Perai.
whet: verb. To sharpen (something, such as a knife or sword) by rubbing on an object, especially a whetstone; to hone.
whig: noun. Acidulated whey, sometimes mixed with buttermilk and sweet herbs, used as a cooling beverage.
whit: noun. The smallest part or particle imaginable; an iota.
white: adjective. Bright and colourless; reflecting equal quantities of all frequencies of visible light.
who: pronoun. What person or people; which person or people; asks for the identity of someone; used in a direct or indirect question.
wig: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Wik-Ngathana.
wight: noun. A living creature, especially a human being.
wit: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Wintu.
wite: verb. To regard (someone) as guilty; to accuse; to blame; to fault.
withe: noun. A flexible, slender shoot or twig, especially when used as a band or for binding; a withy.
woe: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Woleaian.
wog: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Wogamusin.
